commonMedications.name: Semaglutide / GLP-1 (Ozempic/Wegovy) [P1]
commonMedications.coverageNote: Medicare Part D covers for diabetes (tier 3). $25 copay typical. Without coverage ~$900/mo. IRA negotiated price TBD.

commonMedications.name: Levothyroxine (Synthroid) [P1]
commonMedications.coverageNote: Medicare Part D generic tier 1; $0-10 copay

commonMedications.name: Atorvastatin 20mg [P1]
commonMedications.coverageNote: Medicare Part D generic tier 1; $0-10 copay

commonMedications.name: Montelukast (Singulair) [P1]
commonMedications.coverageNote: Medicare Part D generic tier 1; $0-10 copay

commonMedications.name: Hydrochlorothiazide (HCTZ) [P1]
commonMedications.coverageNote: Medicare Part D generic tier 1; $0-10 copay

commonMedications.name: Olmesartan/Medoxomil (Benicar) [P1]
commonMedications.coverageNote: Medicare Part D generic tier 1-2; $4-15 copay

commonMedications.name: Albuterol inhaler [P1]
commonMedications.coverageNote: Medicare Part D tier 2; $10-25 copay. IRA cap applies.

commonMedications.name: Metformin 500mg [P2]
commonMedications.coverageNote: Medicare Part D generic tier 1; $0-10 copay

commonMedications.name: Allopurinol (gout) [P2]
commonMedications.coverageNote: Medicare Part D generic tier 1; $0-10 copay

commonMedications.name: Lisinopril [P2]
commonMedications.coverageNote: Medicare Part D generic tier 1; $0-10 copay

commonMedications.name: Topiramate (Topamax) [P2]
commonMedications.coverageNote: Medicare Part D generic tier 1-2; $4-15 copay

commonMedications.name: Metoprolol (Lopressor) [P2]
commonMedications.coverageNote: Medicare Part D generic tier 1; $0-10 copay

commonMedications.name: Atorvastatin 20mg [P2]
commonMedications.coverageNote: Medicare Part D generic tier 1; $0-10 copay

medicine.pharmacyAccess: CVS, Walgreens, Walmart, Kroger pharmacies widely available. $4 generic programs at Walmart/Kroger. Mail-order via OptumRx, Express Scripts, or Mark Cuban Cost Plus Drugs.
medicine.notes: US prices shown with Medicare Part D coverage. Without insurance, use GoodRx or Cost Plus Drugs for significant savings on generics. IRA Inflation Reduction Act caps insulin at $35/mo and out-of-pocket at $2,000/yr starting 2025.

plans.name: T-Mobile 55+ Essentials
plans.notes: 2 lines unlimited talk/text/data. FL wireless tax ~8% + federal USF. 5G included.

categories.name: Dining Out
categories.seniorDiscounts: Many restaurants offer 10-15% senior discounts. AARP members get discounts at Denny's, Outback, and others.
categories.notes: Lunch for 2 at moderate restaurant ~$35 + tax + 20% tip = ~$44/meal, 2x/week (~8.7/mo) = ~$355/mo in Quincy, FL.

categories.name: Cable + 1 Gig Internet
categories.seniorDiscounts: Some providers offer senior plans. Check for 5-year price lock promotions.
categories.notes: Gig internet $60-80/mo depending on provider and market. Includes basic cable or streaming bundle.

categories.name: Amazon Prime
categories.seniorDiscounts: Medicaid recipients qualify for Prime at $6.99/mo.
categories.notes: $14.99/mo includes Prime Video, free shipping, Prime Reading.

categories.name: Gym/Fitness
categories.seniorDiscounts: SilverSneakers free gym membership through many Medicare Advantage plans.
categories.notes: Planet Fitness $10-25/mo, YMCA $30-50/mo. SilverSneakers covers many gyms free.

categories.name: Parks/Outdoors
categories.seniorDiscounts: America the Beautiful Senior Pass $20/year (lifetime $80) for national parks.
categories.notes: Free parks, trails, and public outdoor spaces.

categories.name: Books/Library
categories.seniorDiscounts: Public libraries free. Many offer senior book clubs and digital lending.
categories.notes: Libraries free. Kindle Unlimited $11.99/mo optional. Budget for occasional book purchases.

categories.name: Coffee/Cafes
categories.seniorDiscounts: AARP discounts at Dunkin'. Some cafes offer senior coffee pricing.

monthlyPass.notes: Quincy transit pass. Seniors 65+ qualify for half-fare on local transit with valid ID or Medicare card.

publicTransit.coverage: Public bus system covers urban core. Limited suburban coverage typically requires a car for errands.
publicTransit.seniorDiscount: Seniors 65+ qualify for half-fare on local transit with valid ID or Medicare card.

rideShare.availability: Uber and Lyft widely available in urban areas.

insurance.notes: Full coverage auto insurance. Senior safe-driver discounts available (5-15%).

parking.notes: Free parking common in suburban areas. Downtown garages higher.

tolls.notes: Estimated monthly toll/highway costs for Quincy area.

carOwnership.seniorDiscounts: Seniors 65+ qualify for half-fare on local transit with valid ID or Medicare card.

transportation.walkability: Moderate walkability in urban core, car-dependent suburbs.

carFree.notes: Transit pass + occasional ride-share. Limited transit — may need more ride-share trips.

withCar.notes: Includes insurance, fuel, parking, maintenance. Moderate suburban costs.

electricVehicle.purchaseNotes: Tesla Model 3 ~$35K, Hyundai Ioniq 5 ~$42K, Chevy Equinox EV ~$28K. Federal tax credit up to $7,500 (income limits apply).
electricVehicle.incentiveNotes: Federal EV tax credit up to $7,500 under IRA (income < $150K single / $300K joint). Used EV credit up to $4,000.

insurance.notes: EV insurance 15-25% higher than comparable ICE vehicle.

maintenance.notes: No oil changes, fewer brake jobs (regen braking). Tires wear faster on heavier EVs.

registration.notes: Many states add EV-specific fees ($100-275/yr) to replace lost gas tax revenue.

electricVehicle.batteryWarranty: 8 years / 100,000 miles (federal minimum). Most manufacturers cover 70-80% capacity retention.
electricVehicle.depreciationNotes: EVs depreciate 40-50% in first 3 years. Budget ~$3,000-5,000/yr depreciation on $35K vehicle.
electricVehicle.totalMonthlyNotes: Includes: charging ($50), insurance ($180), maintenance ($50/mo avg), registration ($17/mo). Excludes purchase/loan payment.

eyeExam.notes: Standard comprehensive eye exam. Most VSP/EyeMed plans cover 1 exam/year with $10-20 copay.

glasses.notes: Frames + single-vision lenses ~$100 with insurance allowance. Progressive lenses add $100-150. Contacts 12-month supply varies by brand.

insurance.coverage: VSP or EyeMed plan, 1 exam + $150 frame allowance/year
insurance.notes: Employer-style standalone vision plan. Covers 1 exam/year, $150 frame allowance, contacts allowance in lieu of frames.

cleaning.notes: Preventive cleaning 2x/year. Most plans cover 100% preventive.

filling.notes: Composite (tooth-colored) filling. Insurance typically covers 80% of basic procedures.

crown.notes: Porcelain-fused-to-metal crown. Insurance typically covers 50% of major procedures.

insurance.coverage: Delta Dental or equivalent, 2 cleanings/year, 80% basic, 50% major
insurance.notes: DHMO or PPO plan. Annual max $1,000-$1,500. Waiting periods may apply for major work.

items.name: Chicken (whole/thighs)
items.notes: Primary protein for both adults & dog

items.name: Ground Turkey / Beef
items.notes: Lean ground for dog food base

items.name: Organ Meat (liver/heart)
items.notes: Essential for balanced home-cooked dog diet

breakdown.notes: Water, sewage, and trash are separate utility bills — see utilities breakdown.

housing.taxNotes: US Southeast does not impose rental tax. Property tax is the landlord's responsibility but factored into rent. Renter's insurance recommended but not legally required.
housing.leaseTerms: 12-month standard lease. Security deposit max 1-2 months rent ($1,125). Pet deposit common ($300-500).
housing.marketNotes: US Southeast rental market. 2BR houses available through Zillow, Apartments.com, and local listings.

stores.name: Publix
stores.notes: Full-service grocery, strong deli and bakery

stores.name: Walmart Supercenter
stores.notes: Lowest everyday prices, grocery pickup available

stores.name: Aldi
stores.notes: Budget-friendly, limited selection, high quality store brands

stores.name: Quincy Farmers Market
stores.notes: Local farmers market, seasonal produce, often Saturday mornings.

healthcare.coverageSummary: Medicare Part A (free) + Part B ($174.70/mo pp) + Medigap Plan G (~$200-220/mo pp) + Part D (~$45/mo pp). IRA out-of-pocket cap $2,000/yr starting 2025.

costs.notes: US Southeast age-rated premium. Higher due to AMD + multiple conditions.
Medicare covers 80%, Medigap covers 20% coinsurance. ~6-8 injections/yr.
OCT scans every 4-8 weeks. Medicare + Medigap covers most.
Quarterly bloodwork/visits. Low copay with Medigap.
Annual visit + as-needed. Mild asthma, infrequent.
US Southeast age-rated premium. Diabetes management but no AMD.
All generics — lower tier plan sufficient
Quarterly A1C, bloodwork. Covered well by Medicare.
Medicare Part B covers glucose monitors. Some copays.

shared.notes: Separate dental plan ~$35/person. Medicare does not cover dental.
Basic vision plan. AMD specialist visits covered by Medicare Part B.
Covered 100% by Medicare — no copay for preventive.

blackHairCare.label: Black Hair Care
blackHairCare.notes: Quincy area salons with experience in natural hair, locs, braids, and protective styles.

whiteHairCare.label: White/Caucasian Hair Care
whiteHairCare.notes: Standard salon and barbershop options throughout Quincy.

mensBigTall.label: Men's Big & Tall Clothing
mensBigTall.notes: DXL is the primary brick-and-mortar option. Online retailers (Amazon, King Size) ship to Quincy.

womensClothing.label: Women's Regular Clothing
womensClothing.notes: Range from budget (Target, Old Navy) to mid-range (LOFT, Banana Republic) available in Quincy area.

shoes.label: Shoes
shoes.notes: DSW and shoe departments at major retailers available in Quincy area. Wide width options at DSW and online.

gym.notes: Planet Fitness ($10-25/mo), LA Fitness, YMCA, and SilverSneakers (free with many Medicare Advantage plans) available in Quincy area.

discounts.name: Medicare
discounts.description: Federal health insurance at 65. Part A (hospital) premium-free for most. Part B (medical) ~$175/month. Part D (drugs) $10-50/month.

discounts.name: AARP Membership
discounts.description: Annual membership $16. Discounts on insurance, dining, travel, retail, and entertainment. Available at age 50+.

discounts.name: US Southeast Social Security Tax
discounts.description: Virginia fully exempts Social Security benefits from state income tax. $12,000 age deduction at 65+.

discounts.name: WMATA/VRE Senior Transit Fare
discounts.description: Reduced fare for riders 65+. Most transit systems offer half-fare or free rides for seniors with ID.

discounts.name: America the Beautiful Senior Pass
discounts.description: Lifetime pass to all national parks and federal recreation areas for $80 (or $20/year).

discounts.name: SilverSneakers Fitness
discounts.description: Free gym membership through participating Medicare Advantage and Medigap plans. Access to 17,000+ locations nationwide.
